notes: #186 has a hard human gate: treasurer must confirm the Every.org fiscal intermediary model is acceptable before work begins. Givebutter is the documented fallback if treasurer rejects it.

#180 is the most complex issue in the batch — it touches schema (ALTER TABLE payments), new server actions (submitPayment, confirmPayment, rejectPayment), Zod validators, member UI (method selector + instruction panels), and admin UI (pending queue). Consider allocating extra time.

#184 is similarly broad — 10 email templates plus cron job plus DB migration plus settings UI. The sub-tickets in the issue body are a natural breakdown: templates first, then wiring, then cron, then preferences.
All 7 external dependencies (#148, #152, #154, #155, #159, #161, #179) are closed and merged. No external blockers.
This is a strict linear chain: 180 -> 184 -> 186. No parallelism is possible within this batch.
